description: A finance-master record defining when payment is due relative to delivery, documents, or shipment. The locked term set is the seven business arrangements both companies use: 100% advance, part-advance + against documents, part-advance + against shipment, 30/70, net 30, net 60, net 90 (plus a Custom escape hatch). A quotation, contract, and PO each carry a payment term; it drives the payment schedule generation, the advance/retention structure, and the DPO analytic. Maintainable individually or by bulk import (natural-key upsert by code).

fields.name: code
fields.label: Payment Term Code
fields.validation: non-blank; uppercase; natural primary key; unique; immutable; the bulk-import upsert key for the payment-terms master
fields.purpose: The stable code a quotation/contract/PO references as its payment term and the natural key bulk import upserts on; links a transaction to its agreed payment arrangement end to end.

fields.name: label
fields.label: Payment Term
fields.validation: human-readable description, e.g. 'Net 30 days from invoice', '30% advance / 70% against documents'
fields.purpose: The display text shown in quotation/PO dropdowns so a buyer selects a term by its plain description rather than a code.

fields.name: termType
fields.label: Term Type
fields.validation: one of the locked set (ontology PaymentTerms enum) plus Custom; drives schedule generation and the advance/retention split
fields.purpose: Classifies the term into the controlled structure the payment engine reads: advance terms create an AdvancePayment, net terms set the due-date offset, part-advance terms create a split schedule. The single field downstream payment logic branches on.

fields.name: advancePercent
fields.label: Advance %
fields.mandatoryCondition: required when termType is 100%Advance, PartAdvance+AgainstDocs, PartAdvance+AgainstShipment, or 30/70
fields.validation: 0-100; 100 for 100%Advance; 30 for the 30/70 term; the prepaid portion of the order value
fields.purpose: The share of order value paid before delivery; sizes the AdvancePayment record (the prepayment asset recouped against later invoices) and the first installment in the generated schedule.

fields.name: netDays
fields.label: Net Days
fields.validation: >= 0; days from the trigger event to the due date; 30/60/90 for the net terms
fields.purpose: The credit period in days the payment due-date offset uses; the input to DPO (days payable outstanding) and the overdue-payment reminder (~28 days) computation.

fields.name: triggerEvent
fields.label: Payment Trigger
fields.validation: one of the set; the event the netDays offset counts from; DOCUMENTS for against-documents terms, SHIPMENT for against-shipment terms
fields.purpose: The event that starts the payment clock so the schedule places each installment correctly (against-documents pays on document receipt, against-shipment on dispatch, net on invoice). Distinguishes the two part-advance terms that share an advance percent but differ on the balance trigger.

fields.name: status
fields.label: Status
fields.validation: INACTIVE not selectable on new quotations/POs; existing transactions retain their term
fields.purpose: Lets an admin retire a payment term from new use without altering historical transactions that already reference it.

invariants: A7 editing a PO's payment term reverts an auto-approved Finance/Management stage to AWAITING_APPROVAL
A9 the generated schedule locks once any installment is APPROVED/PARTIAL_APPROVAL/PROCESSED
A13 audit + SSE on every committed master change
term set is the locked ontology PaymentTerms vocabulary plus Custom
